name: TM01 Work Up
description: Raises the user's Attack and Special Attack by one stage each. A versatile setup move useful for mixed attackers.
method: Given by Captain Ilima after meeting him on Route 2

name: TM06 Toxic
description: Badly poisons the target, causing damage that increases each turn. One of the most useful status moves for stalling strategies.
method: Found on the first floor of Aether Paradise during the story visit

name: TM10 Hidden Power
description: A special move whose type depends on the user's IVs. Can be any type except Normal and Fairy.
method: Given by an NPC inside the Hau'oli City mall

name: TM13 Ice Beam
description: A powerful Ice-type special move with 90 power and 10% chance to freeze. Essential coverage move for many special attackers.
method: Found inside Mount Lanakila cave on the way to the Pokemon League

name: TM24 Thunderbolt
description: A powerful Electric-type special move with 90 power and 10% chance to paralyze. One of the best special coverage moves in the game.
method: Found on the ground in Poni Plains

name: TM26 Earthquake
description: A devastating Ground-type physical move with 100 power. Hits all adjacent Pokemon in doubles. One of the best physical moves in the game.
method: Found deep inside Resolution Cave on Poni Island

name: TM29 Psychic
description: A powerful Psychic-type special move with 90 power and 10% chance to lower the target's Special Defense. Great coverage for Fighting and Poison types.
method: Found on the second floor of Aether Paradise

name: TM31 Brick Break
description: A Fighting-type physical move with 75 power that also breaks Light Screen and Reflect barriers.
method: Found deep inside Verdant Cavern, accessible after completing the trial

name: TM36 Sludge Bomb
description: A Poison-type special move with 90 power and 30% chance to poison. The best Poison-type special attack, great for Fairy coverage.
method: Found inside the Shady House, Team Skull's base in Po Town

name: TM42 Facade
description: A Normal-type physical move with 70 base power that doubles to 140 when the user is burned, paralyzed, or poisoned. Pairs well with Guts ability.
method: Found on Route 9 on Akala Island

name: TM45 Attract
description: Infatuates the target if it is the opposite gender, reducing its chance of attacking to 50%. Does not work on genderless Pokemon.
method: Obtained from an NPC in the Hau'oli City mall

name: TM46 Thief
description: A Dark-type physical move with 60 power that steals the target's held item if the user isn't holding one.
method: Found in the back room of Verdant Cavern after completing the trial

name: TM48 Round
description: A Normal-type special move with 60 power. In double battles, if an ally also uses Round, the second one hits immediately with doubled power.
method: Given by an NPC in Hau'oli City

name: TM53 Energy Ball
description: A Grass-type special move with 90 power and 10% chance to lower the target's Special Defense. Solid Grass-type coverage option.
method: Found inside Lush Jungle on Akala Island

name: TM55 Scald
description: A Water-type special move with 80 power and 30% chance to burn. The best Water-type move for its burn utility, used on almost every Water-type competitively.
method: Given by Captain Lana after completing her trial at Brooklet Hill

name: TM65 Shadow Claw
description: A Ghost-type physical move with 70 power and high critical hit ratio. Great for physical attackers needing Ghost-type coverage.
method: Purchased from the TM shop in Konikoni City

name: TM80 Rock Slide
description: A Rock-type physical move with 75 power and 30% flinch chance. Hits both opponents in doubles, making it extremely useful.
method: Found in Melemele Sea, accessible via Lapras Paddle

name: TM84 Poison Jab
description: A Poison-type physical move with 80 power and 30% chance to poison. The best physical Poison-type move, excellent for hitting Fairy types.
method: Found on Route 17 on Ula'ula Island

name: TM99 Dazzling Gleam
description: A Fairy-type special move with 80 power that hits both opponents in doubles. The most widely distributed Fairy-type special move.
method: Found deep inside Vast Poni Canyon

name: Normalium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Normal-type moves into the Z-Move Breakneck Blitz. The power depends on the base move used.
method: Reward for completing Captain Ilima's trial and defeating the Totem Pokemon

name: Fightinium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Fighting-type moves into the Z-Move All-Out Pummeling.
method: Reward for defeating Island Kahuna Hala in the Melemele Grand Trial

name: Waterium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Water-type moves into the Z-Move Hydro Vortex.
method: Reward for completing Captain Lana's trial and defeating Totem Araquanid

name: Firium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Fire-type moves into the Z-Move Inferno Overdrive.
method: Reward for completing Captain Kiawe's trial and defeating Totem Alolan Marowak

name: Grassium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Grass-type moves into the Z-Move Bloom Doom.
method: Reward for completing Captain Mallow's trial and defeating Totem Lurantis

name: Electrium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Electric-type moves into the Z-Move Gigavolt Havoc.
method: Reward for completing Captain Sophocles's trial and defeating Totem Vikavolt at Hokulani Observatory

name: Rockium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Rock-type moves into the Z-Move Continental Crush.
method: Reward for defeating Island Kahuna Olivia in the Akala Grand Trial

name: Ghostium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Ghost-type moves into the Z-Move Never-Ending Nightmare.
method: Reward for completing Captain Acerola's trial and defeating Totem Mimikyu at the Thrifty Megamart (Abandoned Site)

name: Buginium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Bug-type moves into the Z-Move Savage Spin-Out.
method: Given by Guzma after defeating him at Route 2 post-game

name: Darkinium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Dark-type moves into the Z-Move Black Hole Eclipse.
method: Reward for defeating Island Kahuna Nanu in the Ula'ula Grand Trial

name: Dragonium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Dragon-type moves into the Z-Move Devastating Drake.
method: Found at the end of Vast Poni Canyon after completing the trial

name: Fairium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Fairy-type moves into the Z-Move Twinkle Tackle.
method: Given by a worker on the second floor of Aether Paradise after the main story events

name: Flyinium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Flying-type moves into the Z-Move Supersonic Skystrike.
method: Found in the Farthest Hollow area of Ten Carat Hill, accessible after getting Tauros Ride

name: Groundium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Ground-type moves into the Z-Move Tectonic Rage.
method: Reward for defeating Island Kahuna Hapu in the Poni Grand Trial

name: Icium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Ice-type moves into the Z-Move Subzero Slammer.
method: Found inside Mount Lanakila cave on the way to the Pokemon League

name: Poisonium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Poison-type moves into the Z-Move Acid Downpour.
method: Given by Plumeria after defeating her on Route 17 during the story

name: Psychium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Psychic-type moves into the Z-Move Shattered Psyche.
method: Found in Haina Desert by following a specific path through the desert maze

name: Steelium Z
description: A Z-Crystal that converts Steel-type moves into the Z-Move Corkscrew Crash.
method: Given by Molayne at the Hokulani Observatory after completing Sophocles's trial

name: Decidium Z
description: A species-exclusive Z-Crystal for Decidueye. Converts Spirit Shackle into the Z-Move Sinister Arrow Raid, a powerful Ghost-type attack with 180 base power.
method: Given by Hau on Exeggutor Island if your starter is Rowlet/Decidueye

name: Incinium Z
description: A species-exclusive Z-Crystal for Incineroar. Converts Darkest Lariat into the Z-Move Malicious Moonsault, a powerful Dark-type attack with 180 base power.
method: Given by Hau on Exeggutor Island if your starter is Litten/Incineroar

name: Primarium Z
description: A species-exclusive Z-Crystal for Primarina. Converts Sparkling Aria into the Z-Move Oceanic Operetta, a powerful Water-type attack with 195 base power.
method: Given by Hau on Exeggutor Island if your starter is Popplio/Primarina

name: Eevium Z
description: A species-exclusive Z-Crystal for Eevee. Converts Last Resort into the Z-Move Extreme Evoboost, which raises all of Eevee's stats by 2 stages each.
method: Given by Kagetora in the Thrifty Megamart on Royal Avenue after defeating all 8 Eevee trainers scattered across Alola

name: Pikashunium Z
description: A species-exclusive Z-Crystal for Pikachu wearing Ash's cap. Converts Thunderbolt into the Z-Move 10,000,000 Volt Thunderbolt, a powerful Electric-type attack with 195 base power and guaranteed critical hit.
method: Given by the woman in Pikachu Valley on Route 4 when you have an Ash Cap Pikachu in your party

name: Fire Stone
description: A fire-patterned stone that evolves certain Pokemon: Growlithe into Arcanine, Eevee into Flareon, and Vulpix into Ninetales.
method: Found in Diglett's Tunnel. Also available at Konikoni City stone shop for 3000 Poke Dollars.

name: Thunder Stone
description: A thunderbolt-patterned stone that evolves certain Pokemon: Pikachu into Alolan Raichu (in Alola), Eevee into Jolteon, and Charjabug into Vikavolt (in some games).
method: Found on Route 9. Also available at Konikoni City stone shop for 3000 Poke Dollars.

name: Water Stone
description: A water-drop shaped stone that evolves certain Pokemon: Eevee into Vaporeon, Poliwhirl into Poliwrath, Shellder into Cloyster, and others.
method: Hidden item in the tide pool area on Route 8. Also available at Konikoni City stone shop for 3000 Poke Dollars.

name: Moon Stone
description: A moon-shaped stone that evolves certain Pokemon: Clefairy into Clefable, Jigglypuff into Wigglytuff, Nidorina into Nidoqueen, Nidorino into Nidoking, and others.
method: Can be found via Poke Pelago Isle Aphun mining. Also held by wild Clefairy (5% chance) or Lunatone.

name: Dusk Stone
description: A sinister dark stone that evolves certain Pokemon: Murkrow into Honchkrow, Misdreavus into Mismagius, Lampent into Chandelure, and Doublade into Aegislash.
method: Found on Poni Wilds. Also obtainable from Poke Pelago Isle Aphun mining.

name: Ice Stone
description: A frozen stone that evolves Alolan Sandshrew into Alolan Sandslash and Alolan Vulpix into Alolan Ninetales. Only found in Generation 7 onwards.
method: Found inside a building in Po Town. Also obtainable from Poke Pelago Isle Aphun mining.

name: Sun Stone
description: A sun-shaped stone that evolves certain Pokemon: Gloom into Bellossom, Sunkern into Sunflora, Cottonee into Whimsicott, Petilil into Lilligant, and Helioptile into Heliolisk.
method: Found on Blush Mountain. Also obtainable from Poke Pelago Isle Aphun mining.

name: Prism Scale
description: A mysterious scale that causes Feebas to evolve into Milotic when traded while holding it. An alternative to the Beauty condition method from older games.
method: Found on Exeggutor Island, accessible from Seafolk Village

name: Metal Coat
description: A metallic coating that evolves Onix into Steelix and Scyther into Scizor when traded while holding it. Also boosts Steel-type moves by 20% as a held item.
method: Given by a hiker in Diglett's Tunnel after finding all the Diglett. Also held by wild Magnemite (5% chance).

name: King's Rock
description: A rock that causes Poliwhirl to evolve into Politoed and Slowpoke to evolve into Slowking when traded while holding it. As a held item, gives moves a 10% flinch chance.
method: Held by wild Hariyama (5% chance). Also obtainable from Poke Pelago Isle Aphun mining.

name: Dragon Scale
description: A thick, durable scale that causes Seadra to evolve into Kingdra when traded while holding it.
method: Found in Vast Poni Canyon. Also held by wild Horsea and Bagon (5% chance).

name: Fishing Rod
description: A rod used to fish for Water-type Pokemon in fishing spots marked by bubbling water. Different spots yield different Pokemon depending on the route and rarity.
method: Given by Captain Lana after completing her trial at Brooklet Hill

name: Shiny Charm
description: A key item that triples the base shiny encounter rate from 1/4096 to approximately 1/1365. Stacks with other shiny-boosting methods like SOS chaining and Masuda Method breeding.
method: Given by the Game Director in the GAME FREAK office in Heahea City after completing the Alola Pokedex (all 403 Pokemon)

name: Z-Ring
description: A bracelet set with Z-Crystals that enables you to use Z-Moves in battle. Crafted by Kahuna Hala from the Sparkling Stone given to you by Tapu Koko. Later upgraded to Z-Power Ring at Ultra Megalopolis.
method: Given by Island Kahuna Hala before the Melemele Grand Trial, crafted from your Sparkling Stone

name: Ride Pager
description: A device that lets you summon Ride Pokemon to help traverse the overworld. Replaces HMs from previous games. Ride Pokemon include Tauros (rock smash), Stoutland (item finder), Lapras (surf), Charizard (fly), and more.
method: Received after defeating Island Kahuna Hala in the Melemele Grand Trial

ridePokemon.name: Tauros Charge
ridePokemon.function: Smash rocks and move quickly on land

ridePokemon.name: Sharpedo Jet
ridePokemon.function: Fast surfing and break rocks in water

name: Rotom Dex
description: A Pokedex inhabited by a Rotom, giving it a personality. It provides a map, Pokemon data, advice, and its Roto Loto feature gives you useful items and boosts. In Ultra Sun/Moon, it can also use Z-Rotom Power for a second Z-Move in battle.
method: Professor Kukui gives you the Pokedex, and a Rotom inhabits it after you choose your starter

name: Amulet Coin
description: A held item that doubles prize money from trainer battles when the holder participates. Essential for accumulating money throughout the game.
method: Talk to the gentleman inside the ranch house on Paniola Ranch
effect: Doubles prize money from trainer battles if the holder enters the battle

name: Lucky Egg
description: A held item that boosts EXP gained by 50% for the holder. Extremely valuable for leveling up Pokemon quickly.
method: Given by Professor Kukui in his lab on Route 1 after registering 50 Pokemon in the Pokedex
effect: Holder gains 50% more EXP from battles

name: Soothe Bell
description: A held item that increases the rate at which a Pokemon's friendship grows. Useful for Pokemon that evolve via friendship like Pichu, Eevee (Espeon/Umbreon), Zubat line, and Alolan Meowth.
method: Given by an NPC on Route 3 near the bridge

name: Leftovers
description: A held item that restores 1/16 of the holder's maximum HP at the end of each turn. One of the most universally useful held items in competitive play.
method: Found on Route 1 using Stoutland Search in the tall grass area. Also held by wild Munchlax (5% chance).
effect: Restores 1/16 of max HP at the end of each turn

name: Choice Band
description: A held item that boosts the holder's Attack by 50% but locks it into the first move used. Essential for physical sweepers in competitive play.
method: Purchased at the Battle Tree for 48 BP (Battle Points)
effect: Boosts Attack by 50% but locks the holder into one move

name: Life Orb
description: A held item that boosts all of the holder's damaging moves by 30% but costs 10% of max HP per attack. A popular choice for mixed attackers who need flexibility.
method: Found inside Resolution Cave on Poni Island
effect: Boosts damage of all attacks by 30% but user loses 10% max HP per attack

name: Focus Sash
description: A held item that prevents the holder from being knocked out in one hit when at full HP, leaving it with 1 HP instead. Consumed after use. Essential for frail sweepers and lead Pokemon.
method: Purchased at the Battle Tree for 48 BP (Battle Points)
effect: Survives any single hit at full HP with 1 HP remaining (single use)

name: Eviolite
description: A held item that boosts Defense and Special Defense by 50% for Pokemon that can still evolve. Makes Pokemon like Chansey, Porygon2, and Doublade extremely bulky.
method: Given by Professor Oak's assistant in the Dimensional Research Lab in Konikoni City
effect: Boosts Defense and Sp. Def by 50% for unevolved Pokemon
